summary: Russell's controversial pole-position lap—seized in the final moments of qualifying after a Verstappen crash—sets up a fraught battle for Sunday's win between Mercedes and Ferrari's Leclerc, with both teams contesting the legitimacy of the front-row order. Extreme heat conditions declared as a race hazard will test tire management and driver endurance across the field, particularly favoring teams with superior cooling strategies and fresh driver stamina. A decisive result here could reshape the championship momentum, with Russell and Leclerc locked in a direct points fight while McLaren lurks close enough to capitalize on any Mercedes-Ferrari collision.
